Title: Tpc1 is an important Zn(II)2Cys6 transcriptional regulator required for polarized growth and virulence in the rice blast fungus

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: p22phox subunit of fungal NADPH oxidase complex; Pro41/Ham-6 family; ER membrane protein
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports appressorium function, septin/F-actin organization, plant penetration, and fungal development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for proper superoxide production in appressoria and for polarized penetration growth; loss reduces ROS in appressoria and abolishes efficient plant infection
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NOXD, down-regulated in Δtpc1, encodes the fungal p22phox-like subunit of the NADPH oxidase complex. 2. ΔnoxD mutants are severely defective in leaf/root infection, penetration, sexual reproduction, and septin/F-actin ring assembly. 3. ΔnoxD shows reduced superoxide production in appressoria but increased signal at hyphal tips, indicating compartment-specific ROS control. 4. NoxD interacts with Nox1 and localizes to ER-associated vesicles and the plasma membrane.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene deletion; genetic complementation with NOXD:mRFP/GFP fusions; NBT superoxide staining; penetration assays; sexual reproduction assay; yeast two-hybrid; fluorescence localization of tagged protein
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: NOXD transcript is down-regulated in Δtpc1 and Δmst12 mycelia
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in conidia and appressoria by functional fluorescent fusion constructs
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native NOXD promoter (constitutive/native context); Rp27 strong promoter (constitutive)
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: ER-associated vesicles; plasma membrane of appressoria and conidia
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Nox1; Tpc1/Mst12 regulate its promoter expression
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Appressorium development on hydrophobic surfaces; salt stress (LiCl, NaCl); Congo Red; oxidative stressors (methyl viologen, H2O2); plant infection assays
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ΔnoxD shows accelerated vegetative growth on CM and increased resistance to some salts and Congo Red, indicating altered stress/growth balance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originally annotated as HAM-6/PRO41 and renamed NOXD based on orthology to fungal p22phox. Not an enzyme or TF; functions as an NADPH oxidase complex subunit.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Zn(II)2Cys6 binuclear cluster fungal-specific DNA-binding transcription factor; predicted NLS
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: reactive oxygen species; carbohydrate reserve; lipid reserve
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS supports penetration-associated cytoskeletal remodeling; glycogen/lipid mobilization supports appressorium turgor and infection
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Appressoria; conidia; germ tubes; hyphal tips
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TPC1 is required for timely glycogen and lipid degradation, proper infection-associated autophagy, and regulation of ROS-related pathways needed for appressorium repolarization and penetration
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TPC1 encodes a fungal Zn(II)2Cys6 transcription factor essential for conidiogenesis, polarized growth, appressorium function, and virulence. 2. Δtpc1 mutants show delayed glycogen and lipid mobilization, altered autophagy, defective turgor, and severe penetration defects. 3. Tpc1 localizes to nuclei and its nuclear localization depends on the Pmk1 MAPK pathway. 4. Tpc1 interacts with Mst12 and directly regulates NOXD expression in vivo while promoting Mst12 binding to the NOXD promoter. 5. Loss of Tpc1 broadly disrupts oxidation-reduction processes and superoxide homeostasis.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent master regulator linking Pmk1 signaling to NADPH oxidase-dependent ROS control and reserve mobilization during infection-related development
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ROS-associated regulation; glycogen degradation; lipid mobilization; infection-associated autophagy; polarized growth
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation; modulation of partner TF DNA-binding affinity
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: NOXD; ATG22; alpha-glucosidase; multiple oxidation-reduction and metabolism genes identified by microarray
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates or positively maintains NOXD expression; broadly promotes expression of multiple genes involved in oxidation-reduction processes, glycogen degradation, autophagy-related functions, and pathogenic development
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: TPC1 and MST12 transcripts are overexpressed when the partner is absent, suggesting compensatory feedback/cross-regulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Pmk1 MAP kinase signaling pathway; autophagy status; infection-related surface cues during germination/appressorium formation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with Mst12 in a regulatory complex on the NOXD promoter
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; cytoplasmic localization in Δpmk1 background
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed/overexpressed in initial stages of spore germination and appressorium development; transcript elevated in Δmst12; nuclear signal enhanced in Δatg1 and Δatg8 backgrounds
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Nuclear in vegetative hyphae, attached conidia, and germinated conidia
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hydrophobic surface-induced appressorium development; nutrient depletion; osmotic stress; plant infection conditions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA insertion mutant and targeted deletion; complementation with native promoter TPC1:GFP; transcriptome microarray (ArrayExpress E-MTAB-4127); ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; yeast two-hybrid; fluorescence localization; glycogen/Nile red assays; NBT staining; pathogenicity assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of TPC1 causes severe growth and virulence defects but also relative tolerance to nitrogen depletion and hyperosmotic stress
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tpc1 itself did not bind the tested NOXD promoter probes in EMSA, but enhanced Mst12 DNA-binding affinity; ChIP supports in vivo association with the NOXD promoter.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Downstream transcription factor in the Pmk1 MAPK pathway
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports appressorium-mediated penetration via NADPH oxidase-dependent ROS processes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MST12 positively regulates NOXD expression and, together with Tpc1, supports ROS-associated penetration development
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Mst12 interacts with Tpc1 and binds the NOXD promoter. 2. Mst12 strongly recognizes one NOXD promoter fragment and more weakly two additional fragments in EMSA. 3. Tpc1 enhances Mst12 DNA-binding affinity to the NOXD promoter. 4. NOXD transcript levels are reduced in Δmst12, supporting positive regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct transcriptional regulator of NOXD, a key determinant of appressorial ROS-associated penetration functions
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ROS-associated NADPH oxidase gene expression during appressorium development
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation/modulation in partnership with Tpc1
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: MST12 transcript is overexpressed in Δtpc1, and TPC1 transcript is overexpressed in Δmst12, suggesting reciprocal compensatory regulation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with Tpc1 in a regulatory complex affecting NOXD promoter occupancy/activity
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Appressorium development and plant infection context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; EMSA; qPCR in Δmst12 and Δtpc1 backgrounds
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulatory gene because Results directly test its interaction with Tpc1 and role in NOXD regulation, which underpins ROS-related metabolism in infection.
